author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Partial resolution: added the shared shape-operation API that StringArray was missing, closing concern #4 from the issue (no shared infrastructure for indexing/slicing/reshaping). Keeping StringArray as a separate type rather than forcing it into Array<String, D> — that path would require unsealing the Element trait in ferray-core, which has implications beyond ferray-strings and was not in scope for this pass.

New shape operations (parallel to ferray_core::Array):

- reshape(new_dim) — consume and rebuild with new dimensions; no data copy since strings are owned.
- flatten() — collapse to 1-D StringArray1, row-major order preserved.
- into_dyn() — convert to StringArray<IxDyn> for runtime-rank use.
- transpose() (Ix2 only) — swap rows/columns with element clone into a fresh buffer.
- get(&[indices]) — row-major multi-index lookup, returns None on rank mismatch or out-of-bounds.

Plus the existing map() and the #520 shape-preservation contract I landed earlier this session (every op preserves D).

8 new unit tests cover: 1-D to 2-D reshape, wrong-size reshape error, 2-D flatten, into_dyn preserves shape, 2x3 transpose hand-verification, transpose involution, get() at 1-D and 2-D with out-of-bounds checks. 102 lib tests (up from 94) + 12 property tests pass. Clippy clean.

Out of scope (tracked as follow-ups if demand appears):
- .npy I/O support for string arrays (concern #2)
- Mixed string/numeric containers (concern #1) — would need a NumericOrStringArray enum or unsealing Element.
- Broadcasting StringArray against numeric arrays (concern #3) — arguably should remain distinct since strings are not arithmetic.
